I bought these to replace my Turtle Beach X41's because even with the Microsoft Adapter for $24.99 I still have a hard time chatting with folks on my XBOX One. If I'm playing Battlefield or COD and I'm not going to be chatting with anyone I go back and use my X41's because I think they sound better then these Stealth 500X.

Pros:
1) I like the surround sound aspect. They sound very realistic with helicopters flying around and bullets whizzing past you.
2) I like how clear I can hear others and they can hear me while playing COD Ghosts (the alien maps) (so far that's the only game I chat in unless I'm in party chat on Battlefield 4 with my friend).
3) I like that they are COMPLETELY wireless. Now when I get out of my gaming chair to go get a snack I don't have to take my headphones off ;o)

Cons:
1) After doing all the downloads and updates and using Turtle Beaches recommendations for playing a "shooter" game the sound has a lot of echo and they sound like you are listening inside of a tin can.
2) There is not a lot of treble in these headphones. There is a lot of bass which sound good but I would have liked to have had more treble in them. When I break a window in Battlefield 4 on my Turtle Beach X41's it sounded exactly like glass breaking. On these there is no tinkling of broken glass. Just a lot of bass. If you turn the volume way up the sound seems to sound better but my ears can't take the volume being that loud.
3) I had to take these to a friends house to use their PC in order to download the updates on both the headphones and the other part because you can't use a MAC to update them.
4) I think I read somewhere that there is only one speaker on each side of these headphones and on my four year old X41's there were two so that may be why I think the X41's sound better to me.

I purchased this game and I have to admit that the graphics are pretty amazing. The worst part of this game is the lack of split screen racing. In previous versions, you can race in split screen mode which is the fun part of being with friends. The PS2 and XBOX 360 versions are 1-2 players locally, so why not the PS3 version?

Bad move in my opinion by EA. For this reason, unless you just want to have some fun without being able to race friends sitting next to you and talking trash, I would not bother buying this game.

I ran into one glitch in the game--well into the game. After I finally located "dreamcatchergames.com" (not dreamcather.com) and sent two e-mails, I was informed by their tech support of a patch. Fortunately the patch did not erase the game as is often the case.
I found the storyline was interesting. The puzzles at the beginning were very logical and integrated well in the story. Once I left the ship and ventured onto the Martian landscape the puzzles were more obscure and movement could be done only in certain directions much of the time. I became VERY tired of having to switch disks to use the hovercraft. I also disliked walking around and around the temple because the movements were awkward.
I did have the game crash to desktop a couple of times--so I recommend saving prior to entering the hovercraft.
The Martian puzzles got progressively less logical and just became a guess--press a random button. I was disappointed with end--not even a "success movie".
All in all it was okay--if you need a game to play and don't expect too much out of it. It will not be on my "favorites" list,

I pre-ordered this game simply because the PS3 version is awesome.In the PS3 version the Create mode enables you to...
1) have 3 layers of depth that allow you to make your created levels more apealing to the eye and more diverse in the veriety of electronics and machines you can create.
2) Be able to easly make any shape of any size in a short amount of time.
3) Not be restricted by the operating system.

Such in not so in the PSP create mode. It only has 2 layers of depth. And it is very difficult to make a shape that you want. (Being an advanced creator I sometimes make very complex polygons and things of that nature) Also the controls are very hard to get used to. By now you should have realized that my primary purpose in LBP is to Create.

On the up side of things. The story levels in this new version are quite exiting. (I doubt that Media Modules only used the controls and items offered to you in the create mode to make their complex levels.) Anyway they are very fun and enjoyable for awhile. Being as early as it is in this new versions history, I'm not suprised at all that there are very few fun community levels. This is understandable due to the restricted tools and ability of the new create mode. So don't expect to be wowed by the imagination of your fellow Sackboys in the version.

So to close I say that If you want this game to be able to create while on the move, I wouldn't recomend it unless you have the patience to handle it. If you just like to play the levels, it's not a bad idea to try this new version out. If you have a PS3 and do not have this game for that particular system I would highly advise you to purchase it!

I hope this review helped you.

This is not what a diablo fan wanted.... this hits highs and lows of the games history..

First off a kind note, this game was meant for the PC and I Love it on the PC. I do however actually enjoy the ability of playing this through a controller, they did successfully make that fun and worth its while. it was slightly clunky, but overall was a good experience for someone whose used to a mouse and key on this game.

However, they let Microsoft manage the servers... .this made it both great and beyond useless at the same time.... I realize blizz can't do it all but the servers being monitored my MS left hacks and cheats easily entering the game and spreading like a virus. Where I don't mind this... is a almost nostalgia feeling of diablo 1 and some of diablo 2 where people had some hacked items, making the game a joke. however on the PC I enjoyed this aspect. I still replayed the game as there were still items i wanted to find and enjoyed being "legit". Maphacks were always my favorite i wasn't always huge on the items being hacked up. every corner i turn in the counsel version there are paragon level who even knows how they made it that far's, and not a one of them understands the grind that really should have been needed to acquire such a level. It's not that i'm hating on these people, some of them are the regular old school diablo players just enjoying the new and slightly nostalgia experiences.

if these servers were "monitored" by blizzard... kept at their standards. I would be more interest in continuous play on this game. I'm going back to enjoying my PC version. sadly hundreds of thousands of us have already bought this and made them all think it was a huge success. and for some people it is and it is fun... it's likely a lot of pc users will shelf this game before they get far however.

This would be a 5 star product if the software wasn't so terrible. iCue is slow, buggy, and crashes on a regular basis. Sometimes the headset wakes up and works in seconds, other times it can drag out to 30+ seconds. If you click on iCue in the system tray to open it, the software might eventually open. Keyword: Might. Eventually. If it does open, moving from screen to screen takes longer than your Grandma would take to run a marathon.

For some reason the percentage of battery life remaining is buried in the settings, and only occasionally shows when connected. It doesn't show at all when charging so you have no idea how charged it is until you unplug it.

The firmware of the headset is version 0.02. Yes, ZERO POINT ZERO TWO. Sometimes the voice prompts play when you hit buttons, sometimes you just get beeps. If you press the mute mic button it mutes, but when you unmute it the sidetone shuts off so it sounds like your mic went dead when it didn't, until you either hit mute/unmute over and over or power the headset off and back on.

Sometimes when fully charged the light on the headset turns green. Sometimes it stays yellow for days, but when you unplug it from charging iCue shows it's at 100% charged but the software was just too buggy to make the light turn green consistently.

The mic is too quiet. Even maxed out it barely registers when you test it unless you have the mic so close to your lips it tickles. The only way to make it a reasonable volume is to install a third party equalizer app named Equalizer APO and boost the Mic gain using it. This means it's a software issue not a hardware one that Corsair hasn't bothered to fix despite threads on it being all over gaming forums.

The only complaint I have about the physical hardware is the buttons for mic mute and power is hard to find by touch as they're smooth and have no ridges or anything else to identify them. This is a 5 star piece of hardware hobbled by 1 star software. Considering this product has been on the market since August 2017 there's no excuse other than Corsair being too cheap and lazy to fix the software.

Go buy something made by a manufacturer who knows how to hire decent programmers, Corsair definitely can't.

I played for about a year, and really enjoyed it most of the time, I can't argue it's not a really fun, in depth game. However, you pay [...] dollars a month, but patches often break the game and/or down the servers. The focus of the game keeps changing, favoring pvp sometimes and pve sometimes. It is designed to be addictive, like a drug it's easily rewarding at first, but later painfully difficult to get rewarded. This can be very dangerous, and you must monitor yourself. After level 70 and some casual raiding, without significant time invested, you may hit a wall and be bored and make little/no progress. The servers are also loaded with bots, paid foreign farmers, racists, homophobes, and general idiots. Like many, many people, I became addicted and ruined my life playing the game, be warned.

This is not a auto enthusiast game... I'm not a Forza psycho either... I do love forza. I like that I can get into a car that not only performs how it actually should, but also handles rather realistic.

HOWEVER! this game just throws curve balls all over the damned place... Somehow when I'm driving their cops SRT viper (GTS at that) I'm slow as s***... but then when I'm driving the Ferrari 458 Italia and I'm for some reason planned out at 189mpg....a freaking viper srt cop BLOWS past me like I'm sitting still...

You will be driving your car (full throttle mind you) and it just decides to plane out and not go faster until you build up N20 to boost ur speed...this is bulls***, and obviously not the actual point of nitrous. Another solid example is the SRT charger... now i've owned a RT charger, i've hit 143 in that car and it was still climbing hard. In this game the SRT.... a faster and 20$k pricer version of what I once owned... topped out around 128, and WOULD NOT accelerate beyond this while i watched racers pull away from me until I gather some N20 again and was able to raise my max speed...just annoying bulls*** like that that has me fed up with the game. I realize this isn't a realistic game, and obviously its a game. But let my car perform something close to as it should at full throttle. If you want nitrous to boost me past realistic limitations than ok. but don't restrict me because of that.

I've had DoW2 for a few weeks now, and the game is going great. I've been playing mainly the Single player campaign for a while. The first few games I played online had some players that had really high ping (over 200# and it seemed sometimes when I attacked them they would not get hurt at all. One of my teammates d/ced early in the game and my other teammate didnt even make any units.

I saw someone wrote a review with 1 star mainly because of not being able to transfer games to other accounts/computers, and that it is stuck to your account. Don't blame DoW2 for it, blame steam. Steam made that for all of its games #that you buy from steam# like that. I do agree that it is a pain to have to install Steam and GFWL but luckily I already had both. You can install steam pretty fast, and while installing you can go create an account.

The games graphics have gotten better since the other DoW games. The game isn't as buggy as the others which i'm happy. #or maybe I just got the game after a few patches) I really enjoyed the rpg feeling in the campaign, and all the squads you can switch around with. The gameplay seems a little more simple, since you don't have to build all your buildings for your stuff. It takes away part of the rts feel, but it made it easier. Overall I really enjoyed this game.

This was one BORING game. Button mashing, button mashing, some stupid dwarf i couldn't care less about, telling me to get something I didn't give a c*&^%^ about. Personally, I couldn't handle all the stupid names. In Skyrim, a sword that burns is called, duh... Sword of Burning... In this game it might be called, lewluiatnbuyhikhnn. As if the writer was saying, look how original and clever I can be... I tossed some scrabble tiles. WTH??? Fae? Is that like Fate except with no "t?" What the what the what about the fateless? What? What does that have to do with anything? You're gonna explain it to me? It'll only take 3 hours while the baddies are knocking at the door?? I have to listen to dorky Dr. Doolittle character dwarf, recite a book in monotone for minutes to even begin to care about what is what? And in the end, did I care? Not at all! Because there was nothing in what the idiot dwarf told me that said I should care because of this reason. I don't care about your stupid woman who left you! I don't care about your husband who's lost! Give me some semblance of reason to CARE! At least look attractive or something!! Why are you telling me this, anyway? I'm a STRANGER!!! Why do I have to do that??? Why the heck do I have to go there? What am I doing here?? Amensia? Really??? How original! Am I suppose to take over this world? Am I supposed to kill the evil one? Who was it I'm supposed to fight???? Mr. awhpiuyetrkj??? I couldn't get past the stupidity after 10 hours. 10 hours! I think I gave it a fair shot! I sold it back the next day for 18$. Bye bye 40$... I would've liked it a lot more I guess if I could kill all the annoying npc's. I guess people who play RPGs have a LOT of imagination and a lot of suspension of disbelief, and a LOT of forgiveness for silliness... I just didn't.
I can't believe the comparisons on websites made between this game and Skyrim. It's no contest. Skyrim is immersive because it is believable even while fighting dragons. This game is NOT imaginative, it's ridiculous, and NOT even entertainingly so. Skyrim has a given purpose, you're supposed to get powerful to kill the evil dragon, because you are destined to do so as the Dragon born. KoA? "Well, you just came back from the dead... Why don't you prance around until you find something to do?" People who liked the combat probably haven't tried just mashing "A". They'll probably kill the beasties faster... At least in games like Batman, it mattered HOW you kill your enemies; your tactics mattered. I didn't feel that in this game. I didn't see anything innovative at all in the combat, just copying of other games. I did read a review with a headline something like RPG nerd's RPG. Maybe I'm not a big enough RPG nerd? Well, I loved Mass Effect, Skyrim, Oblivion, Fallout.. Skyrim, you're in a world; in this game you are in a huge corridor full of annoying florescent things and totally-ineffectual people I really didn't care a bit about. I just couldn't handle it. The characters were too silly. The story was nonexistent or copied. Things that could've been explained in 2 words were turned into long drawn out speeches that meandered like your mom telling how she met your dad for the 551st time, except Fred, your uncle, is named Fredrickasonian, look how clever I am!!! I could've forgiven it, if it was meant to be silly, like in Bard's Tale or something. Nope. Meant to be serious, but just turned silly. Don't send me comments... I won't read them... it's my opinion.

I ordered this game but then I decided I better try the demo and I was shocked to find that the graphics are blurry as hell. Obvious 720p or similar upscale with heavy blurring. The gameplay feels alright but the refusal of EA to speak about the resolution of the game left a very bad taste in my mouth so I cancelled the order. Some of their statements in press releases border on outright fraud given their refusal to confirm what resolution the game is running at during gameplay. I literally wasted 90 minutes of my time searching the internet for some confirmation of the gameplay resolution on either next-gen console and found nothing but EA run-arounds at every turn. Honestly, if they had just been willing to confirm that the game was 720p and described what additional features we were getting because of that compromise, I would have gone for it, but the cagey lack of information cost them the sale. Just tell the truth!

Hope they can do a better and more transparent job next year. Obviously core gamers are not their target audience right now. Rather its whoever they can get to spend the most on Ultimate Team... *eyeroll*

I was thoroughly underwhelmed with this game. From invasive dual layer DRM (Steam and Windows live) to uninspiring game play it fails to impress.

You get the game and you have to suffer through STEAM. This is bad enough, but then you have to suffer through another level of misery with the Microsoft overlord poking its nose into your life. I don't want a Microsoft live account, nor do I need one. At least Steam has some advantages (auto updates, game downloading if damaged, etc) while microshaft just wants to sell me more of their crap.

The cover part of the game mechanics system works poorly as your troops will simply stand around getting slaughtered if their cover is destroyed. Assault troops are useless as they are suppressed by normal troops during the assault or will fail to follow up assaults and shoot mindlessly while getting massacred by another nearby group of troops that they will shoot at instead of assaulting. Constantly micro managing your mindless troops gets old quickly, it is like managing a lethal daycare.

On the plus side it only cost me a few bucks as I resisted the urge to get it early. Overall the game damaged the brand, I enjoyed the first one quite a bit but if this is the direction the franchise is gonna take I do not think I will be buying any more.

Strange heading for a five star rating I know, so let me explain.
About the Collectors edition stuff,
The statue is really really awesome I love it, I'm confused as to why they gave the game in a plastic case instead of just putting it with the soundtrack in the steal case which has space for it. It's misses out on the potential of a great art book though.

About the gameplay,
The game still plays like core tekken, this is great, this is fantastic, it's the reason I still give it full stars. As a player that started with tekken 2 and played all right up to tag 2, I'm right at home. New is the rage arts and rage drive, the rage arts are like any modern fighting game super specials complete with cut scene type presentation, what's different is that it's accessible when health is low as opposed to fulling a bar, I welcome this feature. The rage drive allows you to crush through attacks, this I'm not a fan of because tekken for me was always about spacing, baiting and timing, not moves that can allow you to go through your opponents attacks. It does also depend on the low health rage mode and is either or when it comes to using rage drives vs rage arts.

About the story,
personally I think it was great but I hated the ending and it isn't really a "conclusion to the Mishima saga" as advertised because there are clearly more fights to be had involving Mishima blood relatives.

About the game modes,
Arcade could've been better with more fights and the cinematic ending I've come to expect, those endings are now story mode character endings
Treasure mode is basically the new go to offline mode, unlimited fights, unlocking customization options via fights and don't have to worry about health as survival modes
Online didn't seem to be working to great at the time of release so I haven't tried it.
Gallery and jukebox stuff i'm really here nor there about.

About my hate feelings,
The character roster, that's it really. I've played Michelle in tekken 2 and Julia in 3,4,5,6 and both tag games I used Michelle and Julia. Unfortunately neither is in tekken 7, this really sucks for me as the game feels so good yet I can't play with the character I want, with the style I like. they've also left out others like Anna, Lei and Zafina to name a few. I'm sure there are many whose favorite characters aren't present and share my feelings.
I now find myself in a situation where I need to find a new character to like and learn to the level my Julia was, I'm not prepared or really have to time for such and endeavor. Also since new tekken 6 characters were left out, it feels like a risk to focus on anyone completely new in this game.
I can rant on this more but I think it's enough.

Conclusion,
So yes, I love this game as it does feel like the tekken I know and love with improvements, but I pulled the unfortunate short straw with the exclusion of Julia. The right DLC would change my entire opinion and make me absolutely, wholeheartedly love this game.

So I'm going to say right away that i'm a big fan of the Xenogames in general. It's the reason I decided now was the time to buy a switch, I'm also 100+ hours in.

That said I love this game, it feels like the xenoblade games before it but the combat does feel improved now that you no longer have to cycle through arts since they are now assigned to face buttons. The chain combos add a very satisfying feel to the combat as well.

A lot of complaints I read was that the combat takes too long, at first I agreed but when you use the combo chains effectively things feel pretty standard for a xenoblade battle.

The complaints of the map is all true, even with updates it's pretty easy to just be totally lost due to elevations mostly. If they add the follow guide thing from Xenoblade chronicles X then everything would be fine.

Another issue i saw people having was the high level beasts roaming around. I'm probably just used to it from xenoblade chronicles x because it doesn't bother me at all. Like X you learn your place in the food chain and almost like hungry shark, as you level up the usually aggressive beasts become humble no longer attacking unless provoked, this makes fetching stuff in previously visited locations easy.

I seem to always be a bit overpowered though so I tend to be able to stroll through new and expected dangerous areas without having to fight which makes things a little boring

Without spoilers, the story is great. In some ways I like it more than the xenoblade 1, X is different for me as it reminds me more of xenogears and xenosaga but its usually consider the weakest story-wise.

Obtaining the rare blades can be a pain though, 100 hours in and it looks like I'm still missing about half, perhaps they are mostly special story related/ quests ones.

Overall great for any fan of the series and new players as well, you don't have to play the others before it.

If you are comparing this to a > $200 keyboard, this may not meet your needs but for the price, this is really a great keyboard. It has become the rage to have a volume nob usually at the top right corner of keyboards these days. That is fine, I guess, but I prefer what Royal Kludge has done with this board using the nob as a switch for wired, 2.5GHz and the three bluetooth options. In my opinion, this is much more useful than a volume nob, especially when I can use the F-keys for volume just as easily. Using the nob to switch the keyboard from one device to another is not only easier than using the normal FN key + number key to switch between devices but it will provide a quick visual cue as to what device is currently active.

This is a gasket mounted keyboard. I am not a huge fan of a springy gasket keyboard. This is not such a keyboard. It takes a pretty good press to see any plate flex. What it does do is mute the sound of your keys. That along with the polycarbonate plate makes this keyboard a great option for a work environment where other cube mates may be annoyed by a loud keyboard.

The Sky Cyan switches are super smooth and creamy. They are Royal Kludge's own branded switch and a great option for those who like a linear switch with a great feel. I prefer a top loaded tactical switch so I did replace the installed ones with something of my own choice but I will likely find a good use for these switches. They really are good. One thing to note, they are really tight in the PC plate and will take some effort to remove.

I gave this a 4 star review but for the price and features, it really would be a 4.5 star keyboard and should be one of your top considerations for a keyboard in its price range.

I have the 2015 version, but I believe that the problems I'm listing will be the same for anyone.
You can search for this on the Web, but a whole lot of people ran into issues with the Shield 9 upgrade, especially those who are running Plex such as myself. Due to really poor version management issues on Plex, the Shield 9 launch was botched and Nvidia was forced to build a hotfix to deal with issues related to the new storage permission model while Plex had to deploy an OTA update. If you're lucky, those will install correctly and get you by, but the fixes still broke existing installations with large libraries. but for me, the installation will still crash the entire box and you're forced to hard reboot it to recover. moving my music library to a separate Plex server was my only solution.
The BIGGER problem though is the sudden discontinuation of support for certain Light Studio games such as Tomb Raider and Borderlands. Even though I have paid for those games, after forced factory reset, I no longer had access to these games. That just shows the extremely immature business model for Nvidia's first party studio and the greed that Nvidia has to try to push people towards the sub-par Geforce NOW service. Neither Google nor Nvidia will refund you for these.
To add insult to injury, the Play Games app is now broken on Shield 9, which breaks the license check for any first party studio games still left on the Play Store. You can find on the web other people who have suffered that same issue. So with these two combined, you can see that first party gaming experience should be avoided AT ALL COSTS.
If you really want a good experience after owning this for a few years, my best advice now is for you to install Emby on either an x86 tiny PC or a Raspberry Pi while getting a Premium, which provides HW transcoding support for BOTH architectures, and a real game console, whose game library support you can rely on for the long term.

First of all, I'll say like everyone else, that the game is amazing. I wasn't sure I'd like it as much as I did but most of it works great.
Back when shadow of the colossus (sotc) 1st came out I remember thinking that this is what I'd like zelda games to be. And in many ways this game reminds me of sotc. Some similarities:
*The horses have a mind of their own which you guide
*Stamina limitations for climbing etc
*Gathering of fruits and animals (lizard tails in sotc)
*The Devine beasts can be liken to colossi
But while sotc was a simple game, zelda botw has taken most of those ideas and evolved into a much more elaborate game.
Weapons and shields break, don't really love it but sometimes you genuinely have to prepare your gear before certain tasks are done.
There are tons of things to gather.
Your preparation can lead to frustrating battles or comfortable victories.
Unfortunately the game is so open that the story is given in recollected memories and early in game discussions. This due to the fact that most of the game can be considered optional content.
Exploration is the heart of this game, there's always something interesting to be discovered or do, some alternative way to defeat a group of enemies... There's always something. It's great how lively this world feels.
A nice touch is how the climate affects you and the various ways in which you counter these effects. Too cool? Spicy food and warmer gear. Thunderstorm? Put away your metal gear. Its all interesting and fun, you sometimes need to explore more and discover new stuff that would make these trips more comfortable.

One note is the learning curve, because it's so open, you can find yourself feeling pretty overwhelmed by the enemies you meet up. While this is mostly due to not being prepared, you can die a lot and meet some challenges.

Update July 2019: I've owned the keyboard a little over 3 years. Customer support hasn't answered and it's been almost a week so I am dropping the review to 2 stars. That and because it is malfunctioning. I had a ticket in with support and their suggestion was to plug it into another computer, which I didn't have at the time so the issue was never resolved. Now I have tried the keyboard in multiple other computers, and the keys send multiple signals when I press them. Hit E once, anywhere from 2 - 10 E's show up, same with almost every key. So typing is a nightmare I'm constantly using backspace. Typing passwords is almost impossible. I do LOVE a mechanical keyboard but having a 2 year warranty, and after paying $170 for your keyboard, watch it die in 3 years sucks. I did order a renewed K70 keyboard from the corsair outlet for $70, because these keyboards are fantastic once you get used them. And I couldn't really go to another brand since I've invested in the corsair mouse, and corsair pad. The CUE to make the RGB colors schemed is also fantastic. The K95 Platinum is the expensive keyboard from them right now, Rapidfire was pretty new when I got it, I'm switching to the LUX for a quieter key click.

UPDATE: Putting the stars back to 4 stars, this keyboard keeps pretty clean, I did have to buy a can of air to shoot some cat hair out ( I could have just pulled a couple keys with the key puller they sent... ) this review is going back to 4 stars because the RGB lighting is cooler than cool, it's ice cold! Thanks to my new graphics card also having RGB I'm rocking a sick red white blue setup between my gear... Keyboard the american flag, mouse white, headphones red, front case fan blue, graphics card red and white... All red is a little intense for me, but most my friends seem to like it the best. Sadly I use an Xbox 360 controller for most of my gaming right now.

UPDATE: Amazon Prime day July 12 2017, I've had this keyboard about a month, new rapidfire switches, I paid like $180, today it is $109. I wish I waited until now....

UPDATE to the UPDATE: The keyboard is nice, and being able to change the colors is cool, I don't have another keyboard with switches so I don't know if these rapidfire switches are that much better than regular MX cherry reds, I wish I saved the $100 and got a regular mechanical keyboard and used money towards a new video card.

UPDATE: After 12 hours of intensive gaming, I love this keyboard even if I need to get used to how sensitive the keys are... I find myself strafing left because my finger is resting too hard on the A key (I'm used to holding it halfway down to get a fast strafe). I am in the processes of ordering a replacement, my volume adjustment makes a horrible squeal when adjusting volume down (sounds like nails on a chalk board). I am leaving this at 4 out of 5 stars because my extra keys do not include 1-6, I received the key puller WASD in the top half of the package, then keys, Q,W,E,R,D,F. Not my most used keys, my W&S keys definitely get worn off the fastest since I run mostly FPS games, then the number keys wear out.

This is only getting 4 out of 5 stars. Pros, fast key response, cool lights. Cons, expensive, keys are too close together (I used to have a partial key next to the A, so I didn't just bump capslock. Also F11 & F12 are really close to the backspace), software is horrible to learn.

The first COD was epic, genuinely fun, and original. COD 2 was a step up. Everything that has come since COD2 has disappointed me. Since when did telling some half-ass concocted over-blown Hollywood-esque story take precedence over gameplay? These games used to be fun...you're thrown into a battle and you complete your objectives, hopefully killing dozens of human beings in the process. Who wants silly heroic cut-scenes every 2 minutes? Who wants a ridiculous michael bay/tony scott type story to follow? COD2 was my favorite because is is free of these things. There is no story to follow, only countless battle levels in various settings and locations with a diverse arsenal, which makes for a lot of replay-ability. This game (and the modern warfare games) are not re-playable. Once you finish it, you toss it aside. It's not memorable, or even that fun. The characters are silly, the story is ludicrous, and the gameplay is buggy and forgettable.

The graphic are mediocre.
The gameplay has gotten worse - since COD1
Too many cut-scenes.
Too much emphasis on the "story".
The maps are boring - the levels are redundant and simple.
The weapons are boring.
The sound is not very good.
I'll take WW2, Korea, or Vietnam over a future-terror-related storyline any day.

Be a GM:

Came in today. Will update after a year of use and see how quality stands up to a k70 rgb corsair (which lasted years and is still going)

Cons:
1.Keys are wobbly; a little too wobbly for me. Not noticeable while using the keyboard but only when i actually look and move the keys around.
2. No volume control (arguably necessary for form factor)
3. Clicky aspect of the keys feels weird when pressed very slowly. I don't notice it when i'm actually using it normally. I've realized I would rather use their tactile but silent keys (i think orange) over these green switches. But i still enjoy the sound I guess, but it is a little pointless for me. The clickiness already from, for example, a k70 rgb is clicky enough without an extra clicky actuation.
4. Space bar is kind of loud, different obvious sound to the rest of the keys.
Pros:
1. RGB is better imo than corsair's.
2. I'm a sucker for the design (bought this keyboard almost strictly for the white)
3. Quickly accustomed to it, can type 90+ wpm with little errors straight out of the box (this might just mean i'm used to the key distance or irrelevant point)

Bottom line, the keyboard is "good enough" for my use. I mainly bought this keyboard for the aesthetics. Clickiness & keys feel a little cheap, so is it worth 115$ ? Arguably so. Buying my k70 rgb 3+ years ago, it was well over 150$. I would recommend this keyboard, unless the tactile feedback is what you don't want.
If you play Nintendo switch. This product is worth getting.

This fit pretty good over my ears. The headset can be flipped either way so mic starts left or right.

I was so excited for this console, and originally thought it was better than the PS4. But it is buggy, having random graphical bugs on the main menu and struggling with backwards compatible games like Halo Reach. I got it so that I could play split screen with my friends on Halo, but I found out after I purchased the console that any players who want to play split screen together need to have Xbox Live Gold in order to play offline, which is absolutely unacceptable. I later traded it in for a Switch to play Zelda.

My 7 and 9 year old grandsons love this game. It's fun and funny.  Starts out easy so players can progress to harder adventures.  Several of the adventures are cleverly set to music and the boys absolutely love them - play them over and over, even after high scoring.  I have to admit all the adventures are entertaining to watch and I like the music too.  The graphics are fantastic and there is an enormous amount of variety in every aspect of the game: characters, situations, background designs, things to collect, rewards, etc.  The boys even found ways to bash each other and the creatures, which gets them laughing and jumping.  It is obvious that a great deal of thought and creativity went into the development.  Big high 5 to the developers of this game!

My girlfriend bought this and it's cool but it's missing some crucial components to integrate with Microsoft's flight simulator. I came to find out Thrustmaster's aren't,  not with out the full kit. I bought these to replace my old $15 Logitech headset which broke after a year and a half after being used and abused. However with these I was going to take much better care of them to ensure they lasted longer. Boy, was I wrong.

Pros: Good microphone quality

Cons: Horrible build quality. There is a 1/4" of cheap plastic holding the earpiece to the headband which broke on my first pair. On my second pair the adapter broke clean off after I pulled it out to move it to another jack on my computer. They are also very uncomfortable because of high clamping pressure and they also made my ears very itchy. Turtle Beaches website is broken half the time so its impossible to get the warranty activated on them.

Please do not buy this headset, That will make me cry. Spend your money elsewhere! Stay away from Turtle Beaches' headsets in general.

I received a Switch Lite for Christmas. I'm not a fan of the color, but I also understand that the gifter really wanted me to have one, and the colors were limited at the time. For awhile I was on nothing but destiny. I still love it. However I swayed away because I needed the change, and variety is important. I started with Dying light, side note here is that if you like a zombie game go get dying light right now. I loved the sh** out of that game. Could've been a bit longer.. I personally got rid of it after completion because I won't wait for or purchase "add on" garbage. because that's all it is.

Back to this game.
Graphically 4/5 I think theres some parts they could've improved but overall it's rather good. the system runs a little rough here and there, but It hasn't effected or bothered my game play yet. The classes are fun to be part of although I immediately changed my classes after getting through the first part with one class. If you've played DA in the past this is essentially a must have to feel complete.
The story is huge and i'm constantly picking up a new quest here and there while trying to complete another one for some fool who can't carry a sword to protect themselves.
I haven't even came to killing a dragon yet (although one killed me because I walked up on its roost and spooked it) and I'm already immersed in and loving this game. The more you get into this game the stronger it will hold you. There's endless areas to explore, things to collect, and evil doers to slay.

The menu system I think could've used a little bit of work as well, although i have grown more used to it. I get a bit mixed up gearing up all 4 of my characters at once trying to remember which one wears what can be stressful. however once you get past the rush style life we're used to and pay attention to what you have, one can carefully plan a magic defense, distance, or CQC defense depending on what area you're coming up on to attack.

I did do this game as a download through xbox live i'm not sure if the disk is any different but something I do love is that I turn on my xbox, watch some netflix or something and when I go back to the game it's loaded where I left, no boot up or anything, simply constantly ready. It's rather nice. It's made doing whatever I want or need to do easy and seamless to switch back to my game.

I've yet to get through the first map that i scouted a town at initially... i foresee many travels in my future. If you're up for a relax, tactical, strategic based game in which you can manage a few different classes at one time, watch reviews, see a twitch, check some youtube, and pick it up. this is NOT a fast paced game in any way.

Ok, so I usually believe that the truth in reviews is somewhere in the middle and those that take the time to write a review have a lot of time and are charged one way or another.

Not the case here folks. It is truly an "OK" device at best as suggested by these reviews, I don't think it should be used all the time (especially in winter when it is not hot and humid out or in your living room), HOWEVER I can see a potential benefit of the god-awful noise the if you live up here in the north like myself and have cool winters (cooler not needed) and humid, hellish summers (will probably be very useful).

I bought this to trick myself into getting some exercise after the birth of my second baby.  This game is really fun and I like the song selection of this version. I have also played this as a multi-player and that's also really fun.  I think I'll be purchasing some of the other versions.

The other razor headset I have lasted me 5 years(and still works). After a few months of light use, the audio is shifting between left and right speaker. If I apply a little pressure to the volume knob, it temporarily fixes the issue. It appears to be a loose connection.
